Nevada County Now - Local News Nevada County Now is a local community news program that is much more than just the average news show. NCN is a community driven program. This program is for locals, created by locals. It can be watched anywhere, anytime, on any device! Current Candidate & Election Information Are you looking to learn about the candidates or propositions on the ballot for the upcoming elections, check out the following videos. Nevada County Media is a place to create, collaborate, and learn NCM is a media hub and production facility available for our community to utilize. It is place to take workshops, engage in a current project, use equipment to create for yourself, and or work with our team to help you create something amazing. NCM turns concepts into projects and helps share them with the world. Learn more about us. Nevada County Media 355 Crown Point Cir, # D Grass Valley, CA 95945 (behind Analog Devices) Call us: (530) 272-8862 Contact us Current Office Hours: Business Office Hours 10am - 4pm Monday - Thursday Production Hours - ALL DAYS/HOURS BY APPOINTMENT ONLY We are available evenings, Fridays, Saturdays & Sundays for paid work and rentals. NCM is closed on some holidays
pages.about: About Us — Nevada County Media Skip to Content Open Menu Close Menu Our Catalog Calendar NCM News Watch or Listen Now Support Us Become a Supporter Learning Opportunities Volunteering Staff Openings Underwriting HorrorFest Fundraiser A Night of Inspiration Fundraiser Thru the Lens Fundraiser Sponsor a Room Fundraiser Contact 0 0 Become a Supporter Our Catalog Calendar NCM News Watch or Listen Now Support Us Become a Supporter Learning Opportunities Volunteering Staff Openings Underwriting HorrorFest Fundraiser A Night of Inspiration Fundraiser Thru the Lens Fundraiser Sponsor a Room Fundraiser Contact 0 0 Become a Supporter Open Menu Close Menu Our Catalog Calendar NCM News Watch or Listen Now Folder: Support Us Back Become a Supporter Learning Opportunities Volunteering Staff Openings Underwriting HorrorFest Fundraiser A Night of Inspiration Fundraiser Thru the Lens Fundraiser Sponsor a Room Fundraiser Contact Become a Supporter Become part of something wonderful! Nevada County Media is a 501c3 non-profit public benefit corporation located in the beautiful Sierra foothills. Nevada County Media (NCM), formerly known as (NCTV) has been serving the community for over 30+ years. We operate a production facility that allows the community a place to create, collaborate and learn. Our distribution channels include broadcast, streaming and on-demand channels. In addition, NCM creates and facilitates projects that go to a theater distribution. All members of the community are encouraged to submit content. We have a hands-on, fast paced internship program, in addition to specialized programs for all ages. We teach and host a variety of classes, workshops, meetups, and events. Our projects are for film, video and audio. Our organization is open to the entire community. We create a variety of in-house projects and programs to aid in the teaching process and provide a service to the community. NCM has a for-hire team for those needing help with private and commercial projects, as well as one-on-one teaching. NCM receives funding from Public Access (PEG), donations, paid services, grants, and fundraising programs. Our mission Our Vision Nevada County Media empowers creativity, storytelling and community engagement by providing education, resources and opportunities through the use of digital media. To create a better community through collaboration and education. Our Team Staff & Volunteers Operations Manager Stephanie Simpson Production Manager Madelyn Roberts Government Production Manager Kyle Wiggins Specialty Production Manager Andrew Haley, Christena McManus & Jay Tausig NCN Producer Mike Oneto NCN Host Lynn Forbes & Paul Haas NCN Field Host Randi Swisley, Chris Quandt, Candice Livengood & Annie Burlingham Office Volunteer Dee Dinelli Marketing Volunteer Jackie Howard Set Design & Costume Volunteer Al Martinez & Ava Graham Board of Directors Board Chair: Ramona Howard Treasurer/Secretary: Rich Fisher Vice Chair/Film Mentor: Catherine Busch Board Member: Jed Dixon Our board meetings are typically held on the 3rd Mondays of each month and begin at 10:30 AM. These are open to the public unless otherwise noted. There may be exceptions to the date/time so please contact us to verify the meeting time. Ambassador: Joey Jordon Ambassador: Kim Ewing Ambassador: Douglas Hooper Join Our General Email List! Sign up with your email address to receive news and updates. First Name Last Name Email Address Join We respect your privacy. Thank you! You’ll get all sorts of great info from us on upcoming events, programming, and more! Nevada County Media 355 Crown Point Cir, # D Grass Valley, CA 95945 (behind Analog Devices) Call us: (530) 272-8862 Contact us Current Office Hours: Business Office Hours 10am - 4pm Monday - Thursday Production Hours - ALL DAYS/HOURS BY APPOINTMENT ONLY We are available evenings, Fridays, Saturdays & Sundays for paid work and rentals. NCM is closed on some holidays

Monthly Quiz TAP Calendar View TAP Calendar Add Your Tahoe / Truckee Event Get Out & Go Multimedia Tahoe Talks: Community Conference Calls Tiny Porch Concerts Moonshine Minutes: On the Radio Membership Become a Moonshine Member Membership Updates Moonshine Ink Members Moonshine Ink is North Lake Tahoe/Truckee’s independent newspaper, on streets since 2002. We produce a monthly print publication with 24,000 readers along with thousands more a month on this website, which is home to digital editions of every print issue , regular regional Covid-19 updates , and exclusive online reporting and multimedia content. Our readers keep us smart, critical, and transparent. Be on the lookout, too, for Moonshine Ink Tahoe Talks , community conversations hosted over video conference on vital topics, and Tiny Porch concerts (currently on pause due to pandemic precautions). Check out our editorial goals for 2021 and email tips to editors@moonshineink.com . Become a Moonshine Member to support us, and register on our site to add comments to articles and add to our TAP Calendar of events . In addition to providing the community with quality in-depth reporting and information, we offer local businesses and organizations an effective and affordable marketing outlet. Email sales@moonshineink.com for advertising inquiries. Committed to keeping Tahoe smart, we cover everything from breaking news to inspiring stories and distribute our paper for FREE on the second Thursday of every month in over 200 locations. Over the years, we have been honored to be recognized with numerous editorial and advertising awards from the National Newspaper Association (let us brag to you about our 2019 and 2020 victories), as well as many local awards, including the following: Meet our team below, and subscribe to E-News for your weekly need-to-know content. #KeepTahoeSmart Meet Our Staff Mayumi Peacock Publisher/Owner Jonathan Grant Culture Editor Sarah Miller Graphic Designer Alex Hoeft News Reporter Megan Ramsey Digital Content Editor Wade Snider Photographer Melissa Siig Opinion Editor/Reporter Nina Miller Copy Editor Mayumi Peacock Publisher/Owner Visit Author Page Jonathan Grant Culture Editor Visit Author Page Sarah Miller Graphic Designer Visit Author Page Alex Hoeft News Reporter Visit Author Page Megan Ramsey Digital Content Editor Visit Author Page Wade Snider Photographer Visit Author Page Melissa Siig Opinion Editor/Reporter Visit Author Page Nina Miller Copy Editor Visit Author Page In Print Advertise Read the Latest Edition Subscribe to Print Edition Subscribe to Moonshine E-News Leave this field empty if you're human: Info About us Connect AI Policy Privacy Policy Support Moonshine Ink Moonshine Ink is independently owned and operated, meaning we are nimble and responsive — beholden to no one but ourselves and our readers. pages.home: Carrington's Fine Wines - Welcome Welcome Cal's Notes Tasting Schedule FAQ Directions Contact Us Guestbook Welcome Cal's Notes Tasting Schedule FAQ Directions Contact Us Guestbook Welcome Cal's Notes Tasting Schedule FAQ Directions Contact Us Guestbook Hours Th 10:30-5:30 F & Sa 11-7 Closed Sun-Wed This form does not yet contain any fields. Carrington’s Fine Wines is a specialty wine retailer located in the quaint mining town of Nevada City, in the California Sierra Foothills. Offering an unparalleled combination of personalized, expert service with a hand-picked selection of exquisite wines, Carrington’s cellar boasts wines that vary in price and vintage from labels including Gaja, O’Shaughnessy, Rodney Strong Brothers, Ridge, McManis Family, and Turley in both 750 ml and large format bottles. Vintage ports, sparkling wines, and dessert wines are also well represented on the shelves. With more than 30 years in the wine business, Cal Carrington is always available to guide you toward a wine that suits your budget and occasion, provide background on a particular vintner or appellation, or obtain a hard-to-find vintage from his extensive cellars. While weekly tasting focuses on red wines, Carrington’s offers a complete selection of varietals. The wine shop, housed in a historic building constructed in 1861, is a popular destination for locals and visitors alike. And don’t let the shop’s relatively small size fool you. Carrington’s boasts allocations from famous winemakers that position it as one of the top-selling specialty wine shops in California. Enter the world of fine wines at Carrington’s. As Cal Carrington says, “I thank you very much.” 242A Commercial Street • Nevada City, California • 95959 530.265.0195

Together, we present displays at the Rood Center, 950 Maidu Avenue, Nevada City. Public art must always strive to be physically and freely accessible. It can be for simple enjoyment or carry many meanings. It can be set in stone, span a wall, be ephemeral or permanent. It can be fantastic or representational; tell a story, lend civic pride, and remind us that though we may be unique in our identities, we are one. Our program is about providing a thoughtful space of belonging for all residents of Nevada County, expressing community values, heightening our awareness, and helping us question our assumptions. Art in Public Spaces acknowledges both our California Cultural Districts, and communities that populate our diverse landscapes. It is for everyone, a form of collective community expression, reflecting our society and opening our civic spaces. If you’ve visited our exhibitions recently, please take our short anonymous survey. 2025 Highlights Past Exhibitions Upcoming Exhibition 2026 Highlights Mobilize | Art Inspiring Activism with Wild & Scenic Film Festival Jan 16 — May 1, 2026 2025 Highlights Creative Capital | Cultivating Community with ASiF Sep 2025 — Feb 2026 Culture in Focus | Celebrating our Cultural Districts with Nevada County Camera Club May — Aug 2025 In March 2025, we assembled a panel of experts to discuss the historical renaming of Johnson Canyon—in case you missed it, check out this video produced by Bitesize Media. Donate Become a member About Cultural Districts Who we are Press Reports Contact Events Happening Now Creatives Meetups Arts & Culture Calendar Sierra Poetry Festival The Business of Art Programs MUSE Magazine Culture Forward Art at the Airport Art in Public Spaces Arts in Education Artist Accelerator Dark Skies Poet Laureate Poetry Out Loud Resources Newsletters Artist Directory Artist Calls Grants Jobs in Arts Cultural Asset Map Contribute Become a member Donate Volunteer Advocate Advertise Nevada County Arts & Culture acknowledges that the original inhabitants of territories now known as Nevada County continue to be home to indigenous peoples. Generations of Washoe and Nisenan tribal communities, east and west, shared boundaries with Maidu peoples and developed deep understandings of these lands. Through ceremony, culture and stewardship our indigenous peoples continue long standing relationships with the land, as well as with water, air, plant and animal beings. By resolution of Nevada County Board of supervisors, Nevada County Arts & Culture is State-Local Partner with California Arts Council, and part of a coalition of County Arts Agencies serving diverse populations in every county across California. A 501c3 not-for-profit organization, Nevada County Arts & Culture facilitates collaborative efforts that promote and sustain the visual, literary and performing arts of Nevada County to advance the cultural, social and economic life of all community members. Nevada County is a preeminent West Coast cultural destination. It is rich in arts and it is unspoiled. Did you know that three Nevada County townships are among a small pilot cohort representing just fourteen State-designated California Cultural Districts ? 2024 Annual Report Our Team Eliza Tudor Executive Director Eliza's experience as an arts leader spans three continents. From producing film and theatre in Australia, to opera, Ancient Greek theatre and ballet in the Sierra, Eliza then moved to the UK to complete her masters in Cultural Policy. Working as Senior Commissioner for the UK's National Health Service in one of London's most majority non-White high poverty areas, she then worked as Senior Development Executive—Arts & Humanities at the University of Oxford, while also serving on the Board of Trustees of Magdalen Road Studios and Rosetta Life – working nationally through the arts with people suffering life limiting illnesses. Eliza served the boards of California Arts Advocates and Californians for the Arts between 2017 and 2024. She currently serves the Advisory Cohort for a national research study to understand the role, impacts and ongoing evolution of arts service, intermediary, and regranting organizations in a post-pandemic world. She serves the Capitol Region Leadership Council for California Jobs First, and the Regional Tourism Advisory Committee for Gold Country under Visit California. Email Eliza Sofia Vivanco Airaghi Grants Manager Sofia (she/her/ella) is a quadrilingual geographer, dancer, educator, and arts administrator from San Francisco. Sofia holds a B.A. in Geography from UC Berkeley with an emphasis in Latin American Studies, and throughout her time there worked on cross-cultural programming at UC Berkeley’s Multicultural Community Center. Sofia joined us as Grants Manager for Upstate California Creative Corps and is currently overseeing the Upstate Individual Artists Fellowship, both programs serving 19 counties across rural Northern California. Prior to this, she was the Program Manager of Youth Art Exchange (YAX), a multidisciplinary arts education organization working with San Francisco public high school students using the arts and design as a tool for civic engagement and youth development. She has worked as a cartographer with the Anti-Eviction Mapping Project’s (Dis)location initiative; as a dancer has performed at ODC Theater, Cuba Caribe Festival, San Francisco Carnaval, Berkeley Dance Project, and in Salvador, Brazil; and she has exhibited work at IDEO, Latinos in Architecture’s Perspectivas exhibit and Book and Wheel’s Moving Art House . Kellie Cutler Truckee Cultural District Program Manager Kellie is Program Manager for Truckee

pages.about: About Us — La Galleria Home Catalog Home Decor Jewelry Clothing & Accessories About Us Contact Policies Menu Home Catalog Home Decor Jewelry Clothing & Accessories About Us Contact Policies La Galleria has been a staple in the local community for over 38 years. We provide treasures from around the world featuring one of a kind gifts for any occasion, any age group, of all prices. In store and soon online you can find jewelry, fashionable clothing and home décor of all kinds. About The Owner Brooke's passion for travel led her to La Galleria in 2002. Previously, she had lived in Scotland, Italy and Thailand and was drawn to the beauty and culture of what lies within La Galleria, Truckee. Brooke soon began managing and in 2007, she and her husband Chad purchased La Galleria Truckee. La Galleria has allowed Brooke to fulfill her desire to travel and meet people from villages around the world and bring their beauty and artistic designs to local communities. Brooke holds a B.S. in Psychology from U.N.R. and a MA in TESOL. In 2015, Brooke partnered with Beau to open La Galleria Reno, bringing her love of travel, culture and art to a whole new community.

Happenings. Explore. Fall Fun Summer Fun Spring Fun Winter Fun Outdoors Dine Drink Wellness Culture Shop Events. Weddings Meetings Story. Gallery. Info. Community Impact. Contact Us Newsletter. Press Shop. Accessibility Pets Covid-19 Careers FINESSED. WE’VE BEEN BUILDING CHARACTER SINCE 1852 Celebrating Grass Valley at its most authentic…unearthed, restored and brought forward into today. Welcome to the Holbrooke Hotel: where modern luxuries blend seamlessly with a historically unique, rustic-yet-refined, one-of-a-kind property. Set in the heart of our bustling town with easy access to restaurants, tasting rooms, shops and historical sights, our meticulously renovated California Historical Landmark offers a true break from the ordinary and an unforgettable stay. So dust off your boots and sit a spell! After serving the community for over 150 years, the Holbrooke Hotel is ready to spend some time with you. RESERVE A ROOM RESERVE A TABLE ROOMS DINING & DRINKS STORY Miracle Bar. A secret grotto at the Iron Door! When the local speakeasy decides to step aside for a flamboyant limited engagement, you might want to have a look. The Iron Door is going on hiatus until January to bring you Miracle® at The Holbrooke! VISIT MIRACLE® WHAT TO DO Experiences. There’s nothing like a stay at the Holbrooke Hotel. But for those looking to further unlock the magic of the Sierra Foothills, we offer some specially priced packages that will enrich your Grass Valley experience! Bask in the glory of the Sierra Nevada Foothills with our latest offers! VIEW OFFERS Explore. Nestled in the Sierra Nevada foothills, Grass Valley offers outdoor adventures, exquisite dining, thriving arts, shopping, and much more to discover. There’s something for everyone in these hills. See our latest activities here! EXPLORE NOW SIGN UP TODAY Receive the latest news and offers from the Holbrooke. The National Exchange Hotel – Iconic Victorian hotel located in Nevada City, CA Skip to content RESERVE Offers Rooms King Suite King Studio King Room Queen Suite Queen Studio Double Queen Queen Room Standard Room Double Full Dine & Drink Lola The Chef Menu National Bar Bar Menu Reserve Your Table Happenings Explore Fall Fun Summer Fun Spring Fun Winter Fun Outdoors Dine Drink Wellness Culture Shop Events Weddings Meetings Story Nevada City Hotel History A New Chapter Nevada City Today Lola Montez Gallery Info Gift Cards Community Impact Newsletter Contact Accessibility Pets Press Careers Offers Rooms King Suite King Studio King Room Queen Suite Queen Studio Double Queen Queen Room Standard Room Double Full Dine & Drink Lola The Chef Menu National Bar Bar Menu Reserve Your Table Happenings Explore Fall Fun Summer Fun Spring Fun Winter Fun Outdoors Dine Drink Wellness Culture Shop Events Weddings Meetings Story Nevada City Hotel History A New Chapter Nevada City Today Lola Montez Gallery Info Gift Cards Community Impact Newsletter Contact Accessibility Pets Press Careers RESERVE Last Night I dreamt I was at the National Hotel A California icon YOURS TO ENJOY Built in 1856 in what was to become the most sophisticated of the gold rush mining camps, the National Exchange Hotel is a treasured piece of Nevada City history. This grand old dame is full of rich colors and patterns, opulent furnishings and lovingly restored original features. Whispers of stories past are to be discovered around every turn. The National Exchange Hotel invites you to spend unforgettable days and nights with her. WHAT TO DO EXPERIENCES There’s nothing quite like staying at the National Exchange Hotel. But for those looking to further unlock the magic of the Sierra Foothills, we offer exclusive packages that will enrich your Nevada City experience! VIEW OFFERS The ROOMS An indulgence of luxury awaits. The spirit of this unique building shines through in each of our 38 rooms. Each chamber like no other, yet all with a fresh take on Victorian elegance and your comfort in mind. SEE OUR ROOMS Our DINING Libations in our historic bar, a cocktail on the balcony overlooking Broad Street, tea in the upstairs Salon, a sumptuous meal at Lola… you’ll find fresh and creative drinking and dining options here at the National Exchange. EXPLORE DINING Reserve Your Table EXPLORE! Nevada City is a bright, bustling town full of rich history, delicious restaurants, lively bars, unique shops, festivals & farmers markets and an eclectic music & arts scene. A quick trip outside of town reveals deep forests, mountain lakes and many scenic trails and byways to discover. Explore our Winter Fun activities here! EXPLORE NOW! Sign Up for Special Offers Fill out this form to receive updates on events, contests and special offers! First Name* Last Name* Zip/Postal Code* Email* I have read and agree to the Privacy Policy . Yes, I would like to receive marketing emails. *Required Sign Me Up! “I never claimed to be famous. notorious i have always been.” Lola Montez Follow The National Exchange Hotel Your Nevada City escape, nestled in the heart of t Join us @lolaatthenash this Thanksgiving for a fiv The National Exchange, from first cup to last call Strike a pose, if you dare. Capture your Halloween THE NATIONAL EXCHANGE HOTEL 211 Broad Street Nevada City, CA 95959 Show on Map 530.362.7605 Signup for offers RESTAURANT HOURS DINNER at LOLA Sunday-Thursday 5 – 9pm Friday & Saturday 5 – 10pm BRUNCH at LOLA Daily 9am – 2:30pm NATIONAL BAR Monday-Thursday 12 – 10pm Friday 12 – 11pm Saturday 9am – 11pm Sunday 9am – 10pm MORE INFO Contact Us Accessibility Newsletter Gift Card Media Inquiries Careers Pets Covid-19 Contact Us Accessibility Newsletter Gift Card Media Inquiries Careers Pets Covid-19 FOLLOW US Facebook-f Instagram VISIT OUR SISTER HOTEL © 2021 Acme Hospitality, Inc. You can setup a ZOOM video appointment, or an in-store private appointment at our ranch. And it is all free! More thorough webstore coming soon! We are in the process of adding all our inventory to a new online store, so the best selection of alpaca dolls, yarns, gift items will be either via a zoom shopping experience, or live at our ranch. Go To TOUR tab and pick a day/time that work best for you. We offer alpaca yarn, natural hand-dyed yarns (from plants in our garden!), hand-knit alpaca garments, Justine Chenel Designs patterns and work, Original alpaca paintings and cards from local artist Katie Wolff, alpaca dolls, alpaca rugs and so much more! No matter the size of your job, we guarantee your complete satisfaction by delivering a quality product at competitive prices with outstanding service every time. Then email us with a detailed description of the project or item(s) you are interested in. We will promptly return your call or email. Thank you. Mountain Forge - Custom Metalwork Welcome to Mountain Forge We are a family owned and operated blacksmith shop in Lake Tahoe. We handcraft custom metal work. Hans Standteiner opened a small blacksmith shop on the banks of the Truckee River in the late 1960's. His sons, Anton "Toni" and Hansi, have since taken over the business and built a state of the art workshop of their own construction and design. We use age-old techniques from the Standteiner's native Austria and seasoned machinery exclusive to our shop. We make everything by hand, including many of the tools we need to craft our unique client designs. Every product we create is an original design crafted as high quality functional art that will never be duplicated. Our smiths can achieve a wide variety of styles, designs, textures and finishes. To see examples, please visit our galleries via the links below.
